extraction.one_line_summary: MARCZYŃSKI Tomasz attacked twice on Puerto de Torcal final climb, escaped solo 21km out, won by over 1 minute while CONTADOR Alberto's late attack distanced FROOME Chris who crashed on descent.
tactical_patterns: CONTADOR Alberto exploited FROOME Chris mechanical/crash on descent by joining breakaway remnants, gaining 40 seconds without traditional summit attack.
MARCZYŃSKI Tomasz's double acceleration on final climb shed ROJAS and FRAILE, proving second attack needed to break determined breakaway survivors.
Team Sky allowed 9-minute gap to 14-rider break before final climb, suggesting willingness to concede stage when no GC threats present.

rider_signals.MARCZYŃSKI Tomasz: positive: Second Vuelta stage win confirms exceptional descending skills after summit attacks, dangerous in medium-mountain breakaways.
rider_signals.CONTADOR Alberto: positive: Still hunting opportunistically in final Grand Tour, 40-second gain shows ability to exploit chaos rather than pure climbing power.
rider_signals.FROOME Chris: negative: Mechanical then crash on descent reveals nervousness under pressure, lost 40s to CONTADOR and 20s to other rivals.
rider_signals.FRAILE Omar: neutral: In 14-rider break but dropped by MARCZYŃSKI's second acceleration on final climb, insufficient punch for stage win.
rider_signals.ROJAS José Joaquín: neutral: Survived breakaway selection until MARCZYŃSKI's final surge, finished second but lacked winning acceleration.
rider_signals.POLJAŃSKI Paweł: neutral: Named in breakaway but tactical role unclear from available commentary.
